description_long: This object is an antique, hand-cranked cement mixer featuring a large, open-topped drum mounted on a sturdy wooden and metal frame. The drum is angled and supported by a robust metal wheel and gear mechanism, allowing it to be rotated manually using a side crank. The frame is constructed from thick wooden beams, providing stability, while the drum and mechanical components are made of dark, weathered metal. A prominent flywheel on one side is connected to the drum, facilitating smooth rotation. The open top of the drum allows for easy loading of materials, and the bottom section appears to guide mixed cement out. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed gears and handles for manual operation.
description: A vintage, hand-cranked cement mixer with a large metal drum, wooden frame, and exposed gears and wheels for manual operation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the angled drum, large flywheel, and gear mechanism on the frame.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the open drum mouth, crank handle, and symmetrical frame structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view dominated by the large flywheel and the curved profile of the drum.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the drum's interior, visible rollers, and the chute-like exit for mixed cement.
